en add the
quotient to the figure of the 10th house (M. C.) to get the figure
of the ilth house. If you add the quotient again to the figure
of the 11th house, you will get that of the J2th house. By
adopting this procedure you will get 4s-10'-28'-48' for the cusp
of the 31th house, and 5^-10-30'-26' for that of the 12th house.
Now you have the figures for the cusps of four houses viz.
the first, tenth, eleventh and twelfth. Now your task is
easy : You will get the cusp of the 7th house by merely adding
6 signs to the figure of the ascendant. So the 7th house will be
0s-10c-32'-4''. Similarly you will get the cusp of the 4th
house (which is also known as Nadir) by subtracting 6 signs
from that of the M. C. So you get 9M0'-27'-10' for the 4th
house. Similary get the 5th house and the 6th house by deduct-
ing 6 signs from the cusps of the 11th and 12th houses respect-
ively. They will be lOMO and 11»-10'-30'-26* Now
only the second, third, eighth and ninth houses remain to be
fixed.
